Defines macros to indicate if `memcpy` and `memset` are implemented in assembly for ARM Thumb-2.

# Purpose
This C header file defines preprocessor directives to determine whether the `memcpy` and `memset` functions are implemented in assembly language. It uses the `#pragma once` directive to ensure the file is included only once in a single compilation. The file checks if the ARM architecture supports the Thumb-2 instruction set by evaluating the `__ARM_ARCH_ISA_THUMB` macro. If the architecture supports Thumb-2, it sets the `MEMCPY_IMPLEMENTED_IN_ASSEMBLY` and `MEMSET_IMPLEMENTED_IN_ASSEMBLY` macros to 1, indicating that these functions are implemented in assembly. Otherwise, it sets these macros to 0, indicating that the functions are not implemented in assembly.
